# FacetViz — chart-generation guide for coding agents

FacetViz is a dependency-free TypeScript/SVG charting library with a declarative
configuration API. Use this file to generate valid, readable, maintainable chart
options. Full API: `docs/API.md`. Executable examples: `docs/examples-data.js`.

## Agent output contract

When asked to create a chart:

1. Identify the analytical goal before choosing a type: comparison, trend,
   distribution, relationship, composition, range, hierarchy, flow, schedule,
   or small multiples.
2. Inspect the data shape, units, cardinality, missing values, and whether x is
   categorical, numeric, or time-based.
3. Produce one complete `ChartOptions` object with `series` present. Do not
   invent option names or chart types.
4. Prefer the simplest chart that answers the question. Add advanced options
   only when the data or request justifies them.
5. Include a descriptive title, axis titles with units, useful series names,
   tooltips, and an accessibility description unless the surrounding UI already
   supplies the same information.
6. State any material assumption, such as interpreting Unix values as
   milliseconds or treating categories as ordered.
7. For application code, create one chart instance and update it. Do not mount a
   new instance into the same container on every data/theme change.
8. When configuration comes from generated JSON or another untrusted source,
   call `validateChartOptions(candidate)` and repair every error before render.
   Use `validation:{mode:'error'}` when later runtime updates must be guarded too.

```ts
import { FacetViz } from 'facetviz';

const chart = new FacetViz(container, options);
// container: HTMLElement or CSS selector string
// options.series is required
```

The default `facetviz` entrypoint includes every renderer. When bundle size is
important, use `facetviz/core` and import each required family before validation
or construction:

```ts
import { FacetViz, validateChartOptions } from 'facetviz/core';
import 'facetviz/series/line';   // line, spline, step
import 'facetviz/series/pie';    // pie, donut
```

Other family paths mirror renderer module names; `column` also registers bar
and butterfly, `range` registers both area-range aliases, and `scatter`
registers jitter. Use `facetviz/series/all` when dynamic configurations may use
arbitrary chart types. If validation reports `chart.type.unknown` with the core
entrypoint, import the matching family before changing the chart configuration.

## Choose the chart type

Use this decision order:

| Analytical goal | Recommended type | Use when | Avoid when |
|---|---|---|---|
| Compare categories | `column` | Few ordered categories, vertical labels fit | Many/long labels |
| Compare categories with long labels | `bar` | Labels read better horizontally | Time trends |
| Rank one measure | `lollipop` or `bar` | A lighter alternative to bars | Stacked totals |
| Trend over ordered/time x | `line` | Exact progression matters | Unordered categories |
| Smooth visual trend | `spline` | Presentation-focused trend | Overshoot could mislead |
| Discrete state changes | `step` | Values remain constant between changes | Smooth measurements |
| Magnitude over time | `area` | One/few series and baseline matters | Many overlapping series |
| Part-to-whole over categories | stacked `column`/`bar` | Compare totals and components | Only one total |
| Percentage composition | `stacking:'percent'` or `marimekko` | Relative shares matter | Absolute values matter more |
| One small part-to-whole | `pie`/`donut` | 2–6 positive slices | Many slices or close comparisons |
| Relationship between two numbers | `scatter` | x and y are continuous | Categorical x |
| Relationship plus magnitude | `bubble` | z is meaningful and nonnegative | Precise z comparisons |
| Distribution of raw values | `histogram` | Raw numeric observations | Already aggregated bins |
| Distribution by category | `boxplot` | Five-number summaries/outliers | Audience cannot read boxplots |
| Show overlapping category values | `jitter` | Individual observations matter | Very large datasets |
| Compare low/high endpoints | `dumbbell` | Before/after or two measures | Long time series |
| Compare two time/category endpoints | `slope` | Few series, usually two x categories | Many crossings/series |
| Uncertainty/range | `arearange`, `columnrange`, `errorbar` | Low/high bounds are primary | Only a single value exists |
| Financial OHLC | `candlestick` | open/high/low/close data | Generic ranges |
| Running contributions | `waterfall` | Deltas build toward totals | Independent categories |
| Progress against targets | `bullet` | Measure, target, qualitative ranges | Multiple time points |
| One KPI on a dial | `gauge` | A bounded single value is expected | Comparing several values |
| Events on a sequence | `timeline` | Event order/timing is primary | Durations matter |
| Tasks with durations | `gantt` | start/end per task | Dependencies/critical path required |
| Category × category intensity | `heatmap` | Dense matrix comparison | Precise individual values |
| Daily activity | `calendar` | Date/value contribution-style grid | Irregular non-daily data |
| Stages narrowing | `funnel` | Ordered conversion stages | Non-sequential categories |
| Hierarchical structure | `treegraph` | Parent-child topology matters | Part-to-whole area matters |
| Hierarchical proportions | `sunburst` | Nested share and hierarchy matter | Precise cross-branch comparison |
| Weighted flows | `sankey` | Acyclic source→target flow | Cyclic graphs |
| Multivariate radial profile | `radar` | Few series and 3–8 comparable metrics | Different units/scales |
| Back-to-back comparison | `butterfly` | Two populations share categories | More than two main series |
| Tiny inline trend | `sparkline` | Table/card context already labels it | Standalone analytical chart |
| Repeated comparable panels | `trellis` | Same measure across dimensions | Non-cartesian cross-tab panels |

Default to `line` for a genuine ordered trend and `column`/`bar` for categorical
comparison. Never choose pie/donut solely because values sum to 100; use it only
when the user needs a simple part-to-whole view.

Supported `ChartType` values (exact spelling):

```txt
line, spline, step, area, areaspline, arearange, areasplinerange,
column, bar, columnrange, waterfall, histogram, bullet, lollipop,
scatter, jitter, bubble, boxplot, dumbbell, slope, candlestick, errorbar,
pie, donut, radialbar, gauge, radar, funnel, sunburst,
heatmap, calendar, timeline, gantt, treegraph, sankey,
butterfly, marimekko, sparkline
```

`radar`, `butterfly`, `marimekko`, and `sparkline` depend on chart-level layout
behavior; set them as `chart.type`, not only as an isolated `series.type`.

## Root options

```ts
interface ChartOptions {
  chart?: {
    type?: ChartType;                         // default 'line'
    width?: number; height?: number;          // defaults to container size, then 640×400
    backgroundColor?: string;
    spacing?: [number, number, number, number]; // top right bottom left
    inverted?: boolean;
    colors?: string[];
    animation?: boolean | { enabled?: boolean; duration?: number; easing?: string };
    zoom?: 'x' | 'y' | 'xy' | false | { type?: 'x'|'y'|'xy'|false };
    reflow?: boolean;                         // default true
    responsive?: boolean;                     // default true
    boost?: boolean | { enabled?: boolean; threshold?: number };
    events?: { render?; click? };
  };
  title?: { text?: string; align?: 'left'|'center'|'right'; style?: Record<string,string> };
  subtitle?: { text?: string; align?: 'left'|'center'|'right' };
  xAxis?: AxisOptions | AxisOptions[];
  yAxis?: AxisOptions | AxisOptions[];
  tooltip?: TooltipOptions;
  legend?: LegendOptions;
  plotOptions?: { series?: Partial<SeriesOptions>; [type: string]: Partial<SeriesOptions>|undefined };
  series: SeriesOptions[];                    // REQUIRED
  colors?: string[];                          // palette; chart.colors has precedence
  theme?: 'light'|'dark'|'high-contrast'|'pastel'|ThemeObject;
  trellis?: { columns?: string; rows?: string; gap?: number;
              sharedX?: boolean; sharedY?: boolean; table?: boolean };
  drilldown?: { series: Array<SeriesOptions & { id: string }> };
  accessibility?: {
    enabled?: boolean;
    description?: string;
    keyboardNavigation?: boolean;             // default true
    pointDescriptionFormatter?: (ctx: {
      seriesName: string; seriesIndex: number; pointIndex: number;
      x: number|string; y?: number; low?: number; high?: number; point: PointOptions;
    }) => string;
  };
  validation?: boolean | {
    mode?: 'warn'|'error'|'silent';
    onIssue?: (issue: { code: string; severity: 'error'|'warning';
                        path: string; message: string; suggestion?: string }) => void;
  };
  seriesEvents?: { click?; mouseOver?; mouseOut?; legendItemClick? };
}
```

### Axis options

```ts
interface AxisOptions {
  visible?: boolean;
  opposite?: boolean;                        // x→top, y→right
  categories?: string[];
  type?: 'linear'|'log'|'category'|'datetime';
  title?: { text?: string; style?: Record<string,string> };
  min?: number; max?: number; tickCount?: number;
  labels?: {
    enabled?: boolean;
    format?: string;
    formatter?: (value: number|string) => string;
    rotation?: number;
    style?: Record<string,string>;
  };
  crosshair?: boolean;
  gridLineWidth?: number; gridLineColor?: string;
  lineWidth?: number; lineColor?: string; ticks?: boolean;
  reversed?: boolean;
  plotLines?: Array<{ value: number|string; color?: string; width?: number;
    dashStyle?: string; zIndex?: 'above'|'below';
    label?: { text: string; align?: 'left'|'center'|'right';
      verticalAlign?: 'above'|'below'; color?: string } }>;
  plotBands?: Array<{ from: number|string; to: number|string; color?: string;
    label?: { text: string; color?: string } }>;
  dimension?: string;
  dimensions?: string[];
  aggregate?: 'sum'|'avg'|'count'|'min'|'max';
}
```

### Series options

```ts
interface SeriesOptions {
  type?: ChartType;
  name?: string;
  data: PointInput[];
  color?: string;
  visible?: boolean; showInLegend?: boolean;
  stack?: string|number; stacking?: 'normal'|'percent';
  xAxis?: number; yAxis?: number;
  lineWidth?: number; size?: number;
  marker?: { enabled?: boolean; radius?: number;
    symbol?: 'circle'|'square'|'diamond'|'triangle'|'rectangle';
    fillColor?: string; lineColor?: string; lineWidth?: number;
    width?: number; height?: number };
  dataLabels?: { enabled?: boolean; format?: string; formatter?: (ctx)=>string;
    color?: string; fontSize?: string; fontWeight?: string;
    position?: 'outside'|'inside'|'center'|'base'|'top'|'bottom'|'left'|'right';
    distance?: number; rotation?: number; backgroundColor?: string };
  tooltip?: { enabled?: boolean; format?: string; formatter?: (ctx)=>string;
    valuePrefix?: string; valueSuffix?: string; valueDecimals?: number };
  states?: { hover?: { enabled?: boolean; scale?: number; brightness?: number } };
}
```

```ts
interface TooltipOptions {
  enabled?: boolean;
  shared?: boolean;
  format?: string;
  formatter?: (ctx) => string;
  backgroundColor?: string; borderColor?: string; color?: string;
  valuePrefix?: string; valueSuffix?: string; valueDecimals?: number;
}

interface LegendOptions {
  enabled?: boolean;
  align?: 'left'|'center'|'right';
  verticalAlign?: 'top'|'bottom';
  layout?: 'horizontal'|'vertical';
  itemStyle?: Record<string,string>;
}

interface ThemeObject {
  base?: 'light'|'dark'|'high-contrast'|'pastel'|string;
  colors?: string[];
  backgroundColor?: string;
  fontFamily?: string;
  title?: { color?: string; fontSize?: string; fontWeight?: string };
  subtitle?: { color?: string; fontSize?: string };
  axis?: { labelColor?: string; titleColor?: string;
    lineColor?: string; gridLineColor?: string };
  dataLabel?: { color?: string };
  legend?: { color?: string; hiddenColor?: string };
  tooltip?: { backgroundColor?: string; borderColor?: string; color?: string };
  neutralColor?: string;
}
```

`plotOptions.series` applies to every series; `plotOptions[type]` applies to
that type; explicit series options win. Use `plotOptions` instead of repeating
identical label, marker, stacking, or tooltip settings.

## Point data shapes

```ts
type PointInput =
  | number                       // y; x comes from category or index
  | [number|string, number]      // [x,y]
  | [number|string, number, number] // [x,low,high]
  | { x?, y?, name?, color?, ...typeSpecificFields }
  | null;                        // line/area gap
```

Rules:

- For categorical series, prefer `xAxis.categories` plus aligned numeric arrays
  when every series has every category.
- Use object or tuple points when categories are missing/reordered. Stacking is
  matched by actual x value.
- For numeric/time x, use `[x,y]` or `{x,y}` and do not stringify x.
- Datetime x values are millisecond timestamps. Use `Date.UTC(...)` when time
  zones should not alter the date.
- Use `null` to create a visible break in line/area data.
- Avoid `NaN`, `Infinity`, mixed units, and duplicate x values unless intentional.

## Exact data for each specialized type

```ts
// column, bar, line, spline, step, area, lollipop, slope, radar, sparkline
data: [5, 3, 8]                         // pair with xAxis.categories
data: [['Jan', 5], ['Feb', 3]]

// range, columnrange, errorbar, dumbbell
data: [['A', 10, 18], ['B', 7, 15]]    // [x,low,high]

// pie, donut, radialbar, funnel
data: [{ name: 'A', y: 42 }, { name: 'B', y: 58 }]

// scatter / jitter / bubble
data: [{ x: 2, y: 8 }]                  // scatter
data: [{ x: 'Group A', y: 8 }]          // jitter
data: [{ x: 2, y: 8, z: 30 }]           // bubble

// boxplot
data: [{ x: 'A', min: 2, q1: 4, median: 6, q3: 8, max: 11,
         outliers: [15] }]

// candlestick
data: [{ x: timestamp, open: 100, high: 112, low: 96, close: 108 }]

// waterfall
data: [{ x: 'Revenue', y: 120 }, { x: 'Costs', y: -45 },
       { x: 'Profit', y: 0, isSum: true }]

// histogram (bins must be a positive integer when provided)
{ type: 'histogram', bins: 12, data: [12.1, 13.7, 11.8, ...] }

// marimekko: one aligned value per category in each series; column width is
// derived from that category's total and each column is 100% stacked
xAxis: { categories: ['A','B','C'] }
series: [{ name: 'Online', data: [30,50,20] },
         { name: 'Retail', data: [20,10,40] }]

// butterfly: exactly two main series mirrored around a shared category axis
xAxis: { categories: ['18–24','25–34','35–44'] }
series: [{ name: 'Group A', data: [18,25,20] },
         { name: 'Group B', data: [16,28,23] }]

// bullet
data: [{ name: 'Revenue', y: 72, target: 80, ranges: [50, 75, 100] }]

// gauge
{ type: 'gauge', min: 0, max: 100, data: [{ name: 'SLA', y: 86 }],
  bands: [{ from: 0, to: 60, color: '#ef4444' },
          { from: 60, to: 85, color: '#f59e0b' },
          { from: 85, to: 100, color: '#22c55e' }] }

// heatmap
data: [{ x: 'Mon', y: 'Morning', value: 18 }]

// calendar
data: [{ date: new Date(2026, 6, 21).getTime(), value: 8 }]

// timeline (events are evenly spaced and x is displayed as its label)
data: [{ x: '2026-01-01', name: 'Launch', description: 'v1' }]

// gantt
data: [{ name: 'Design', start: Date.UTC(2026,0,1), end: Date.UTC(2026,0,12) }]

// treegraph / sunburst
data: [{ id: 'root', name: 'Company' },
       { id: 'sales', parent: 'root', name: 'Sales', value: 30 }]

// sankey: positive weights and an acyclic graph are required
data: [{ from: 'Visit', to: 'Trial', weight: 240 },
       { from: 'Trial', to: 'Paid', weight: 80 }]
```

Type-specific series options:

- `pie`/`donut`: `innerSize:'60%'`; optional `dimensions:['parent','child']`;
  point `z` creates variable-radius slices.
- `bubble`: `sizeRange:[6,34]`.
- `jitter`: `jitter:0.5` is spread in category-band units.
- `boxplot`: `boxColors:{lower,upper,median,whisker,border}` and
  `outlierMarker`.
- `dumbbell`: `lowColor`, `highColor`, `connectorColor`, `connectorWidth`.
- `radar`/`area`: `fillOpacity` where applicable.
- `sparkline`: `sparkline:{last?,min?,max?}`.

## Option-selection heuristics

### Titles and axes

- Title the question or measure, not the chart type: “Monthly revenue”, not
  “Column chart”.
- Put units in axis titles: `Revenue (USD)`, `Latency (ms)`, `Conversion (%)`.
- Use `xAxis.type:'datetime'` for time values and `crosshair:true` for dense
  trends.
- Rotate category labels only when necessary: `-45` first, `-90` as a last
  resort. Prefer `bar` for many long labels.
- Do not set `min`/`max` unless the analytical range is intentional. Explicit
  bounds are exact and can clip data.
- Use a log axis only for strictly positive values spanning orders of magnitude.
- Use `plotLines` for targets/benchmarks and `plotBands` for meaningful ranges;
  use `zIndex:'above'` when marks could cover a reference line.

### Color and theme

- Prefer `theme` or a root palette over repeated series colors.
- Built-ins: `'light'`, `'dark'`, `'high-contrast'`, `'pastel'`.
- `chart.colors` overrides top-level `colors`, which overrides theme colors.
- An explicit `series.color` intentionally opts that series out of later palette
  changes.
- Use consistent color for the same concept across charts. Do not rely on color
  alone for meaning; names, labels, or position must carry the distinction.
- Use `high-contrast` and an accessibility description when accessibility is a
  priority.
- SVG points are keyboard navigable by default: Tab enters once; Arrow keys move
  between points; Home/End jump; Enter/Space activate. Preserve this behavior
  unless the surrounding application supplies an equivalent interaction model.
- Use `accessibility.pointDescriptionFormatter` when domain context or units are
  not clear from the generated series/category/value label.

### Labels, legend, and tooltip

- Enable data labels for sparse summary charts, not dense line/scatter charts.
- Prefer `dataLabels.position:'inside'` only when marks are large enough.
- Stacked bars default labels to the segment center; this is usually correct.
- Use `tooltip.shared:true` for aligned multi-series x values with the same
  unit. For dual-axis series with different units, prefer individual tooltips.
- Use `legend.enabled:false` for a single obvious series. Point-legend charts
  (`pie`, `donut`, `radialbar`) still benefit from a legend when labels are not
  shown.
- Default tooltip formatting safely escapes substituted data. A custom
  `tooltip.formatter` returns trusted HTML; never insert untrusted strings into
  it without escaping.

Formatting tokens for labels/tooltips: `{x}`, `{y}`, `{name}`, `{series}`,
`{percentage}`, `{total}`, `{low}`, `{high}`, `{point.<field>}`. Axis labels use
`{value}`. Examples: `{y:$,.1f}`, `{y:.0%}`, `{y:,.1s}`, `{x:%b %d}`.

### Performance and responsiveness

- Leave `chart.reflow:true` for normal responsive layouts.
- Call `chart.reflow()` after a hidden tab, grid, or resizable panel becomes
  visible or finishes layout.
- Boost auto-enables beyond 1500 points for supported cartesian line/point
  series. Do not force it for small charts.
- Boosted canvas pixels are embedded into SVG/PNG exports.
- Disable animation in tests, server-generated screenshots, or rapid update
  loops: `chart:{animation:false}`.

## Composition patterns

### Grouped and stacked columns

Series without `stacking` group side-by-side. Apply stacking to every series in
the same stack.

```ts
{
  chart: { type: 'column' },
  title: { text: 'Quarterly revenue by region' },
  xAxis: { categories: ['Q1','Q2','Q3','Q4'], title: { text: 'Quarter' } },
  yAxis: { title: { text: 'Revenue (USD)' } },
  tooltip: { shared: true, valuePrefix: '$', valueDecimals: 0 },
  plotOptions: { column: { stacking: 'normal' } },
  series: [
    { name: 'North', stack: 'revenue', data: [12,15,14,18] },
    { name: 'South', stack: 'revenue', data: [9,11,13,14] },
  ],
  accessibility: { description: 'Quarterly revenue split between North and South regions.' },
}
```

### Datetime trend with zoom

```ts
{
  chart: { type: 'line', zoom: 'x' },
  title: { text: 'Daily response time' },
  xAxis: { type: 'datetime', crosshair: true, title: { text: 'Date' } },
  yAxis: { title: { text: 'Response time (ms)' } },
  tooltip: { shared: true, valueSuffix: ' ms', valueDecimals: 1 },
  series: [{ name: 'p95', data: [[Date.UTC(2026,6,1),128], ...] }],
}
```

### Combo chart with a secondary axis

```ts
{
  chart: { type: 'column' },
  xAxis: { categories: months },
  yAxis: [
    { title: { text: 'Orders' } },
    { title: { text: 'Conversion (%)' }, opposite: true },
  ],
  series: [
    { name: 'Orders', data: orderCounts },
    { type: 'spline', name: 'Conversion', yAxis: 1, data: conversionRates,
      tooltip: { valueSuffix: '%' } },
  ],
}
```

### Nested categories

```ts
{
  chart: { type: 'column' },
  xAxis: { dimensions: ['Region','Category'], aggregate: 'sum' },
  yAxis: { title: { text: 'Sales' } },
  series: [{ name: 'Sales', data: [
    { Region: 'East', Category: 'Tech', y: 12 },
    { Region: 'East', Category: 'Office', y: 8 },
  ] }],
}
```

### Small multiples

```ts
{
  chart: { type: 'line' },
  trellis: { columns: 'product', rows: 'region', sharedX: true, sharedY: true },
  series: [{ name: 'Sales', data: [
    { x: 'Jan', y: 12, product: 'A', region: 'East' },
    { x: 'Feb', y: 15, product: 'A', region: 'East' },
  ] }],
}
```

Cross-tab trellis (`table` default `true`) is for cartesian types. For pie,
donut, sunburst, gauge, and other non-cartesian types, use
`trellis:{columns:'field',table:false}` or separate chart instances.

### Drilldown

```ts
{
  chart: { type: 'column' },
  series: [{ name: 'Products', data: [
    { name: 'Hardware', y: 40, drilldown: 'hardware' },
  ] }],
  drilldown: { series: [
    { id: 'hardware', name: 'Hardware', data: [['Laptops',25],['Desktops',15]] },
  ] },
}
```

## Live API

```ts
chart.update(partialOptions);            // deep-merge, re-resolve, render
chart.setData(seriesIndex, data);         // preserve runtime visibility
chart.addPoint(seriesIndex, point);
chart.appendData(seriesIndex, points, { maxPoints: 2_000 });
chart.batchUpdate((batch) => {            // synchronous; one final render
  batch.appendData(0, pointsA, { maxPoints: 2_000 });
  batch.appendData(1, pointsB, { maxPoints: 2_000 });
});
chart.setSize(width, height);
chart.reflow();
chart.drillUp();
chart.on('point:click', listener);         // returns unsubscribe function
chart.getSVG();
chart.downloadSVG(filename?);
chart.downloadPNG(filename?, scale?);
chart.toPNGBlob(scale?);
chart.legendItems;
chart.hasLegend;
chart.destroy();
```

For live or streaming charts, prefer `appendData()` over repeatedly copying the
entire data array into `setData()`. Set `maxPoints` to an explicit positive
integer so long-lived dashboards have bounded memory; it limits raw source rows
before rendering. Wrap related series/options changes in `batchUpdate()` so
validation, rebuilding, and rendering occur once. The callback
must be synchronous. Batches can nest, and thrown callbacks roll back their
level. Streaming preserves series visibility and active single-axis zoom.

For palette/theme changes, use `chart.update({theme})` or
`chart.update({colors:[...]})`; existing cartesian and point-based series are
recolored. Keep a reference to the chart and call `destroy()` when its UI owner
unmounts.

## Validation checklist

Before returning generated chart code, verify:

- `series` exists, every series has `data`, and every `type` is supported.
- `xAxis.categories.length` matches aligned numeric arrays.
- Every range has `low <= high`; every boxplot has
  `min <= q1 <= median <= q3 <= max`.
- Pie/funnel/radial values and Sankey weights are positive where required.
- Histogram `bins` is a positive integer.
- Sankey and hierarchy IDs are valid; Sankey links are acyclic.
- Datetime x/start/end values are milliseconds and Gantt `end > start`.
- Secondary-axis series use `yAxis:1` and top-level `yAxis` has two entries.
- Stacked series share the intended x values and stacking mode.
- Percent data is not accidentally multiplied twice (`.0%` multiplies by 100;
  use it for ratios such as `0.42`, not values already equal to `42`).
- Log axes contain no zero or negative values.
- Data labels will not overwhelm the marks.
- Long category labels use `bar`, rotation, thinning, or fewer categories.
- Colors have adequate contrast against `chart.backgroundColor`.
- The accessibility description explains the key measure and grouping.
- Custom point descriptions include domain meaning and units when the defaults
  are insufficient.
- `validateChartOptions()` returns no errors. Treat its stable `path` as the
  field to repair and its `code` as the machine-readable failure category;
  warnings should be reviewed but do not make the configuration invalid.
- Application code updates or destroys an existing chart rather than stacking
  multiple SVG roots in one container.
- Library changes that touch rendering, layout, point normalization, or boost
  preserve the automated budgets: 1,200 SVG points, 100k boosted points, and a
  100k live update must each complete under one second in the browser suite;
  boosted cases must remain below 200 DOM nodes. The bundled library must stay
  below 280 kB raw and 70 kB gzip.

## Common mistakes to avoid

- Do not use `chart.type:'bar'` together with manual negative values merely to
  make bars point left; `bar` already means horizontal orientation.
- Do not use categorical string x values for a continuous scatter/time axis.
- Do not set `yAxis.min:0` on boxplot, dumbbell, candlestick, or range charts
  unless zero is analytically meaningful; these types auto-pad their extent.
- Do not add a zero baseline manually for `column`, `bar`, `area`, `areaspline`,
  `errorbar`, or `lollipop`; they already include it.
- Do not use `trellis.table:true` for non-cartesian charts.
- Do not enable markers and data labels for thousands of line points.
- Do not hard-code a series color if it should respond to palette/theme updates.
- Do not rely on legend color alone to explain a chart.
- Do not pass raw HTML from untrusted data through a tooltip formatter.
- Do not guess unsupported options from another charting library; consult
  `docs/API.md` when an option is not listed here.
